observations: Rhythm scorer on v4b puts comma_period_ratio at 0.596, sentence_length_mean at 11.0, and sentence_length_cv at 0.763; all three sit inside the global target range.
Openers are still repetitive: entropy is 6.18 against a 7.2 floor, with 'The' opening 63 sentences and 'She' opening 29.
One-sentence paragraphs remain high at 30.7% against a 15-24% range, especially in the cellar testimony and trader exchange.
Long-sentence percentage runs 16.8%, above the 7-14% target band; the chapter alternates clipped command beats with a few overextended description strings rather than a steadier mix.
Texture remains the main rhythm failure: participial 5.5%, semicolon 0.5%, texture score 17.2. Flagged zones cluster in P48-P56, P66-P76, P110-P128, and P131-P138.

fixes.rule: texture desert — connect clipped diagnostic fragments
fixes.before: 'She's clean,' Jaina said. 'Dry, hungry, bruised. The plague never reached her.'
fixes.after: 'She's clean,' Jaina said. 'Dry, hungry, bruised, but clean. The plague never reached her.'

fixes.rule: emphasisRule — consecutive fragment emphasis without enough buildup
fixes.before: Sylvanas gave Miri a few minutes. Enough for the shaking in her hands to ease. Enough for her breath to stop skittering at every shadow across the trapdoor.
fixes.after: Sylvanas gave Miri a few minutes, enough for the shaking in her hands to ease and her breath to stop skittering at every shadow across the trapdoor.

fixes.rule: telegram prose — stacked short declaratives in witness summary
fixes.before: People stopped eating. They stopped getting out of bed.
fixes.after: People stopped eating and stopped getting out of bed.

fixes.rule: texture desert — fragment stack in trader entrance
fixes.before: Open-bed farm wagon. Slatted sides. Canvas rolled back and lashed. Grain dust and old chaff silvered the joints.
fixes.after: An open-bed farm wagon came first: slatted sides, canvas rolled back and lashed, grain dust and old chaff silvering the joints.

fixes.rule: paragraph cadence — bare fragment opener before long descriptive sentence
fixes.before: Four riders. Darker farmland ahead, fallow fields edged with bare hedgerows, the road flattening as it left the river valley.
fixes.after: Four riders now, with darker farmland ahead, fallow fields edged with bare hedgerows, the road flattening as it left the river valley.

observations: Narration stays inside Sylvanas's tactical field vocabulary at lines 3-23, 67-85, and 171-189: lanes, spoor, warning, column, dispatch, patrol, and border do the work.
No therapy-speak, corporate phrasing, or modern bureaucratic diction survived the pass; the chapter avoids words from the style guide's banned lists.
Jaina's spoken register stays field-practical rather than lab-report clinical at lines 47-64 and 171-175: well water, root cellar floors, trough, layer, and ground that answers.
Miri's testimony at lines 131-151 stays child-broken and sensory instead of turning into tidy exposition about contamination mechanics.
Residual abstraction remains mild at line 57 in 'Something brought it in'; it reads as spoken uncertainty rather than document voice, so it was flagged but left in voice.

fixes.before: Jaina rubbed grain dust from one thumb with the heel of the other hand, as if the answer lived in the grit.
fixes.after: Jaina rubbed grain dust from one thumb with the heel of the other hand.

fixes.rule: vague emotional carrier instead of concrete physical read
fixes.before: The girl's fingers locked harder in the cloak every time she said the word bread, as if that alone might call something back.
fixes.after: The girl's fingers locked harder in the cloak every time she said the word bread, knuckles whitening at the sound of it.

fixes.before: The child had both hands buried in the horsehair at the nearest neck, as if warmth mattered more than talk.
fixes.after: The child had both hands buried in the horsehair at the nearest neck, taking warmth where talk would not help.

fixes.before: He kept searching the lane for the missing people as if some of them might still step out with coin in hand.
fixes.after: He kept searching the lane for the missing people who should have stepped out with coin in hand.

observations: Metaphor density stays sparse, roughly 0.7 per thousand words by manual count; the chapter does not drift into ornamental comparison.
No forbidden wall, armor, burden, storm, drowning, shattered, or light-versus-dark metaphor appears anywhere in the chapter.
The few figurative turns stay operational rather than decorative: line 3 prices the village like a command problem, and line 213 measures reduced margin like arrow count after a fight.
No paragraph stacks multiple metaphors, and no extended metaphor runs more than a sentence.
Imagery remains concrete and visualizable throughout the Millhaven sweep, the cellar, and the trader scene; the prose describes objects, tracks, bodies, and weather more often than it compares them.
Paragraphs stay short and pressure-driven, which suits a village sweep and witness interview, but the chapter over-relies on single-sentence blocks.
No paragraph exceeds the style guide's hard cap; there are no 7-8 sentence briefing walls and no 100-word narration bricks that need splitting.
The chapter now preserves causal movement without internal hard cuts: granary to spoor, spoor to cellar, and proof to dispatch all flow directly.
Dialogue paragraphs mostly stay at 1-2 sentences, while narration paragraphs in the granary and road-cut scenes stay in the 2-4 sentence workhorse band.
Scene-end paragraphs at lines 271-281 land on motion rather than summary, matching the preferred decision-to-consequence transition convention.
Said/asked attribution lands at 43.3% of dialogue paragraphs, squarely inside the 40-50% target.
Creative tags are at 0%; the chapter correctly avoids murmured, intoned, purred, growled, and similar style-guide violations.
Action-beat attribution lands at 31.7%, below the 35-45% target, most noticeably in the trader exchange where dialogue runs faster than body language.
Untagged dialogue lands at 25.0%, above the 10-20% target; the cellar exchange stays clear, but the trader scene leans too heavily on unattributed turns.
Beat placement is otherwise healthy: before-speech and mid-speech action beats dominate lines 99-125, 199-213, and 227-245.
Sylvanas's narration stays clipped and tactical at lines 5-23, 67-85, and 171-189, with exits, track quality, border risk, and rider count always foregrounded.
The chapter never names Sylvanas's emotions directly; concern, pressure, and urgency are rendered through command choices, crouching over spoor, and dispatch calculus.
Physical positioning remains meaningful in every scene: threshold listening, house-by-house sweep, crouched ground read, back-from-the-barricade restraint, and Jaina drawing up at Sylvanas's left.
Her sensory signature stays auditory and spatial early on: silence, river sound, horse reaction, street geometry, and approach lines all arrive before color or sentiment.

The only notable voice drift is global texture underperformance in the later dialogue-heavy pages; Sylvanas's clipped mode is accurate to character but pushes the chapter below the style guide's connective-tissue floor.
Estimated adverbs per page are 1.25, below the hard cap of 2 per page.
Estimated passive voice is 4.2%, well below the 8% ceiling.
Filter verbs banned in Sylvanas POV do not appear in the chapter; there is no noticed/observed/realized scaffolding.
Simile load is now compliant on the hard count that mattered most: 'as if' constructions fell from 4 to 0, while plain 'like' stays at 3 for the whole chapter.
Show-versus-tell stays strong: the village is rendered through cold stoves, mold, dragged tracks, cellar stink, horse behavior, and rider logistics rather than narrator summary.
Exposition integration passes: plague mechanics enter through Jaina's field readings and Sylvanas's spoor work at lines 43-64 and 157-175, not through narrator lecture.
Power dynamics track the spec in Sylvanas/Jaina exchanges: Sylvanas gets short declaratives at lines 39-64 and 199-203, while Jaina's answers run longer and more qualified.
Scene shape is clean: Scene 1 escalates from silence to saturation to missing dead, Scene 2 turns on the cellar witness, and Scene 3 converts proof into dispatch and southbound commitment.
Speculative integration stays domesticated; magic is handled as field infrastructure at lines 45-52 and 165-175, not spectacle or awe.
Humor is sparse but still permitted for chapter 3: Neral's horse-temper lines at 105 and 207 offer brief dry relief without breaking the dead-village tone.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass - Lines 3-23, 69-85, and 171-189 stay military-precise, concrete, and profession-filtered; no abstract emotional vocabulary enters her narration.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass - Default cadence stays short-to-medium and declarative, but the chapter's global texture still underperforms in later dialogue-heavy zones.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass - Metaphor use stays below 1 per 1000 words and remains tactical rather than pretty.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass - Emotion appears as command pressure, stillness, dispatch choices, and tactical proximity, never as named feeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass - Sylvanas thinks in threat assessment, warning thresholds, and route logic throughout the village read and dispatch decision.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass - Auditory and spatial cues lead the chapter: silence, river noise, horse reactions, track quality, lane width, and road direction.

ruleViolations: Global texture metrics fall short in P110-P128 and P131-P138; Sylvanas's clipped command cadence is in voice, but it still leaves the prose below the connective-tissue floor.
One-sentence paragraph percentage remains above the global paragraph-cadence target, especially once the chapter turns into dispatch and trader dialogue.

metrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 22.7:77.3 overall (1002 dialogue words / 3410 narration words); below the chapter-range target, with the strongest dialogue density concentrated in the cellar witness scene.

flaggedZones.instruction: Add participial links and one compound sentence inside the cellar entry so action, smell, and positional reads connect instead of landing as separate beats.
Thread Miri's testimony with a few connective clauses or ', Ving' constructions around the witness handling beats, while preserving her broken spoken rhythm.
Join at least one of the short witness-reaction sentences with a dependent clause so the emotional read sits inside motion instead of on isolated stubs.
Use one compound clause around the north-road confirmation to keep Sylvanas's clipped voice without stacking single-hit confirmations.
Enrich the dispatch-to-trader handoff with participial connectors and one earned semicolon in narration where two closely related tactical thoughts currently split apart.
Blend the trader's surprise beat into the surrounding physical description with one connective clause before the question lands.
Collapse one of the short market-memory sentences into a longer spoken line or follow-up beat so the exchange stops clicking from fragment to fragment.
Add compound narration around the southbound recommitment so the closing road picture carries more connective tissue before the final line.
Replace one or two isolated short trader-exit sentences with joined clauses using ', and' or ', with' constructions.

textureFindings.priorityInstruction: Participial density is 5.5% against a 12.0% target and the texture score is 17.2 against a 27.0 target — add ', Ving' connective tissue in the flagged cellar and trader zones before worrying about any other metric.
